response: {"name":"eCLIP-seq data processing","description":"Computational pipeline for processing eCLIP-seq sequencing data from raw reads through demultiplexing, adapter trimming, genome alignment, PCR duplicate removal, peak calling, and downstream analyses including motif finding and cluster annotation.","data_type":"sequencing","raw_data_source":null,"steps":[{"step_number":1,"description":"Standard HiSeq demultiplexing followed by custom demultiplexing of eCLIP libraries with distinct inline barcodes; the random-mer (UMI) is appended to the read name for later PCR duplicate removal.","input_data":"Raw FASTQ reads from HiSeq sequencing with inline barcodes","output_data":"Demultiplexed FASTQ files with random-mer appended to read names","software":"Custom scripts (Illumina HiSeq demultiplexing + custom inline barcode demultiplexing)","software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":2,"description":"Adapter trimming of demultiplexed reads; reads shorter than 18 bp after trimming are discarded.","input_data":"Demultiplexed FASTQ files","output_data":"Adapter-trimmed FASTQ files (reads ≥18 bp retained)","software":"cutadapt","software_version":"v1.9.dev1","parameters":{"min_read_length":"18"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":3,"description":"Mapping of trimmed reads against human repetitive elements in RepBase to segregate repeat-mapping reads for separate analysis.","input_data":"Adapter-trimmed FASTQ files","output_data":"Reads mapped to RepBase (repeat-mapping reads) and unmapped reads for further processing","software":"STAR","software_version":"v2.4.0i","parameters":{"reference":"RepBase v18.05 (human elements)"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":4,"description":"Mapping of non-repeat reads against the full human genome (hg19) including a splice junction database to obtain uniquely mapping reads.","input_data":"Non-repeat FASTQ reads from step 3","output_data":"Uniquely mapped reads (BAM files aligned to hg19)","software":"STAR","software_version":"v2.4.0i","parameters":{"reference_genome":"hg19","annotation":"splice junction database"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":5,"description":"PCR duplicate removal using a custom script that removes duplicate reads sharing identical Read1 start position, Read2 start position, and random-mer sequence, yielding 'Usable' reads.","input_data":"Uniquely mapped reads (BAM files)","output_data":"PCR-deduplicated 'Usable' reads (BAM files)","software":"Custom PCR duplicate removal script","software_version":null,"parameters":{"dedup_criteria":"identical Read1 start position + Read2 start position + random-mer sequence"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":6,"description":"For eCLIP datasets with multiple inline barcodes, usable reads from individual barcodes are merged into a single dataset at the usable-read stage.","input_data":"Usable reads from multiple inline-barcoded eCLIP libraries","output_data":"Merged usable reads BAM file","software":null,"software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":7,"description":"Cluster/peak identification on usable reads using CLIPper, considering only Read 2 (enriched for termination at the crosslink site).","input_data":"Merged usable reads BAM file","output_data":"CLIPper peak/cluster calls","software":"CLIPper","software_version":"1.0","parameters":{"species":"hg19","options":"-s hg19 -o -bonferroni -superlocal --threshold-method binomial --save-pickle","read_considered":"Read 2 only"},"code_reference":"https://github.com/YeoLab/clipper/releases/tag/1.0"},{"step_number":8,"description":"RPM normalization of read tracks against the total number of usable reads in each dataset for visualization on the UCSC Genome Browser.","input_data":"Usable reads BAM files","output_data":"RPM-normalized genome browser tracks","software":"UCSC Genome Browser (custom normalization)","software_version":null,"parameters":{"normalization":"RPM (reads per million) against total usable reads"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":9,"description":"Downsampling analysis performed by 100 iterations of randomly permuting uniquely mapped reads, selecting top N reads, and applying PCR duplicate removal to identify usable reads at each depth.","input_data":"Uniquely mapped reads (BAM files)","output_data":"Usable read counts across downsampling depths (100 iterations per depth)","software":"Custom downsampling script","software_version":null,"parameters":{"iterations":"100","note":"For iCLIP multi-library samples, only the library with the highest percent usable was used"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":10,"description":"De novo motif finding for RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP clusters using HOMER's findMotifs program, with cluster sequences compared against background regions matched by genic region type.","input_data":"CLIPper-identified cluster sequences (RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP)","output_data":"De novo RNA motifs enriched in CLIP clusters","software":"HOMER findMotifs","software_version":null,"parameters":{"threads":"4","rna":"true","num_motifs":"10","motif_lengths":"5,6,7,8,9","background":"3 random same-sized regions per cluster matched by genic region type (e.g., introns, 3′UTRs)"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":11,"description":"Cluster location annotation: pie charts generated by counting total bases covered by peaks for each genic region type.","input_data":"CLIPper peak/cluster calls","output_data":"Cluster location pie charts showing distribution across genic region types","software":"Custom annotation script","software_version":null,"parameters":{},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":12,"description":"For RBFOX2 RNase I shearing analysis, each cluster is annotated by overlapping genic region using a priority-ranked region scheme, and peak counts per region type are tallied.","input_data":"CLIPper peak/cluster calls (RBFOX2 RNase I shearing eCLIP)","output_data":"Per-region peak counts for RBFOX2 clusters","software":"Custom annotation script","software_version":null,"parameters":{"region_priority":"same as region-level analysis priority order"},"code_reference":null},{"step_number":13,"description":"Motif enrichment analysis before and after SMInput normalization: CLIPper-identified clusters are extended to a minimum of 100 nt centered around cluster midpoints for RBFOX2, PUM2, TARDBP, and TRA2A.","input_data":"CLIPper-identified clusters for RBFOX2, PUM2, TARDBP, and TRA2A","output_data":"Extended cluster sequences (≥100 nt) centered on cluster midpoints for motif enrichment analysis","software":"Custom script","software_version":null,"parameters":{"min_extension":"100 nt","centering":"midpoint of CLIPper-identified
